rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sandra Pierce'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2018, Line 1. Defendant Sandra Pierce's Demurrer To Complaint. SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to properly allege his causes of action in compliance with CRC 2.112. The Complaint, as plead, is uncertain due to Plaintiff's failure to comply. It appears, but it is not clear, that Plaintiff is alleging at least 3 distinct contracts pertaining to (1) proceeds from refinance and sale of Plaintiff's real property; (2) earning, e.g. paychecks; and (3) mortgage/tax contributions towards Defendant's real property. Breach of each contract is to be separately alleged. Fraud causes of action, if any, also to be separately alleged with requisite specificity. Allegations in the complaint are sufficient to show that the statute of limitations has not started running until April 2015; or was tolled until that time. Additionally, if Plaintiff wishes to proceed with the claims pertaining to refinance/sale proceeds, Plaintiff shall address the subject matter jurisdiction issue raised by Defendant. $187,574.56 held by Defendant for Plaintiff (to be spent at Plaintiff's direction only) is the property of the bankruptcy estate per 11 U.S.C. 541. Plaintiff had equitable interest in this sum per the terms of the oral agreement. Plaintiff may petition the Bankruptcy Court for permission to proceed with this claim. In the alternative, Bankruptcy Trustee may seek leave to substitute in as a Plaintiff for the claim pertaining to this sum. In the alternative, Plaintiff may amend the Complaint omitting any claims pertaining to refinance/sale proceeds. Therefore, Plaintiff shall have 90 day leave to amend in order to address the matter with the Bankruptcy Court. This Court does not make a determination regarding judicial estoppel argument at this time. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Deft And Cross Complainant To Expunge Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2018, Line 3. DEFENDANT EDMUND CHUTE'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens. Moot. Moving party does not cite any authority for the proposition that a withdrawn Lis Pendens is subject to expungement. Attorney's fees granted in the amount $4,675 to be paid within 15 days. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE WAN ET AL VS. RICHARD PADILLA ET AL

DEFENDANTS RICHARD PADILLA, DONALD BRANCHFLOWER, WILLIAM CARMICHAEL, JENNIFER EMPERADOR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer.
